verdict.text: Santo Domingo scores Caution. The Dominican Republic capital has a tourist-accessible zone — Zona Colonial (a UNESCO World Heritage Site), Piantini, and the Malecón waterfront — alongside genuinely dangerous barrios. All three advisories note the DR at elevated caution.

reconciliation.text: Robbery, pickpocketing, and some violent crime occur even in tourist zones. The Zona Colonial by day is manageable; after dark it requires more vigilance, especially away from the main pedestrian streets. Barrios like Ciudad Nueva (just behind Zona Colonial) and Los Alcarrizos carry much higher risk.

Stay in Piantini, Naco, or hotels near the Malecón for a safer base. Visit Zona Colonial during daylight and return by app before dark. The DR's primary tourist draw (Punta Cana, Puerto Plata) operates at significantly lower risk than the capital.

Official statistics: World Bank / UNODC — national intentional homicide rate for Dominican Republic: 2023: 10.9/100k; 2022: 12.4/100k; 2021: 10.5/100k; 2020: 8.7/100k. Note: this is a country-level rate, not city-specific. Source: World Bank Open Data (VC.IHR.PSRC.P5), compiled from UNODC Crime Trends Survey.
